back_translation: In the third year of the reign of King Belshazzar, a vision appeared to me, Daniel, after the one that had appeared to me earlier.
In the vision I saw myself in the citadel of Susa, which is in the province of Elam; and in the vision I was beside the Ulai Canal.
I lifted my eyes and looked, and behold, a ram with two horns was standing beside the canal. The two horns were long, but one was longer than the other, and the longer one came up later.
I saw the ram charging toward the west, the north, and the south. No animal could stand against it, and there was no deliverance from its power. It did as it pleased and became great.
As I was contemplating, behold, a male goat came from the west, crossing the whole earth without touching the ground; and the goat had a prominent horn between its eyes.
It came to the two-horned ram I had seen standing beside the canal and rushed at it with furious power.

I saw it reach the ram; it was enraged at it, struck the ram, and broke its two horns. The ram had no power to stand against it, so the goat threw it to the ground and trampled it, and no one could deliver the ram from its power.
So the male goat became very great, but when it was at full strength, the great horn was broken, and four prominent horns came up in its place, pointing toward the four winds of heaven.
From one of these horns came forth a little horn, which grew very great toward the south, the east, and toward the Beautiful Land.
It grew great, up to the host of heaven, and it cast down some of the host and some of the stars to the earth and trampled them.
It magnified itself, even up to the Prince of the host; it took away the daily sacrifice from Him and overthrew the place of His sanctuary.
The host was given over to it together with the daily sacrifice because of rebellion; it cast truth to the ground and prospered in all it did.

Then I heard a holy one speaking, and another holy one asked the one who was speaking, 'How long will the vision last—the daily sacrifice, the rebellion that causes desolation, and the surrender of the sanctuary and the host to be trampled?'
He answered me, 'For 2,300 evenings and mornings; then the sanctuary will be cleansed and restored to its proper state.'
When I, Daniel, had seen the vision and was trying to understand it, behold, one having the appearance of a man stood before me.
And I heard a man's voice from the middle of the Ulai calling out, 'Gabriel, explain the vision to this man.'
So Gabriel came near to where I stood. When he came, I was terrified and fell facedown. But he said to me, 'Son of man, understand that the vision concerns the time of the end.'
While he was speaking with me, I fell into a deep sleep with my face to the ground. But he touched me and helped me to my feet.
And he said, 'Behold, I will make known to you what will happen in the latter time of wrath, for the vision concerns the appointed time of the end.
The two-horned ram that you saw represents the kings of Media and Persia.
The shaggy male goat is the king of Greece, and the great horn between its eyes is the first king.
As for the broken horn and the four that came up in its place: four kingdoms will arise from that nation, but not with its power.
In the latter part of their reign, when the rebels have reached their full measure, an insolent king, skilled in intrigue, will arise.
His power will be great, but not by his own strength. He will cause terrible destruction and prosper in all he does; he will destroy the mighty and the holy people.

Through his cunning he will make deceit prosper in his hand, and in his heart he will magnify himself. In a time of peace he will destroy many, and he will rise up against the Prince of princes. Yet he will be broken, but not by human hands.
The vision of the evenings and mornings that has been told is true, but seal up the vision, for it concerns many days yet to come.
And I, Daniel, was exhausted and lay ill for days. Then I rose and carried out the king's business. I was appalled by the vision, and no one could understand it.
